meta.title: Leadership Safety Walk / Gemba — Walk Record & Tracked Commitments
meta.subtitle: Night-shift warehouse pick-face
meta.author: HSE Leadership
meta.classification: Internal — worker concerns role-labelled — competent-person review required
meta.deid_notice: Worker concerns are recorded against role/group labels only (psychological safety). No concern is attributed to a nameable individual; identifiers were scrubbed before drafting and any re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document. This is an engagement walk record, not an inspection checklist.

sections.text: This is the record of a leadership safety walk (felt leadership / genchi-genbutsu — 'go and see') on the night-shift warehouse pick-face. It is an engagement walk built on OPEN conversation prompts — not a tick-box inspection. Purpose: felt-leadership engagement and hazard-spotting. Framed against ISO 45001:2018 clauses 5.1 (leadership and commitment) and 5.4 (consultation and participation of workers).
Open prompts used (engagement, not a checklist)

items: Felt-leadership / engagement: "What's the one thing that makes your job harder or riskier than it needs to be?"
Felt-leadership / engagement: "What safety improvement would you make if it were your call?"
Hazard-spotting: "Walk me through what could go wrong in this task."
Control-verification: "What controls do you rely on here, and have any ever failed you?"
Prompts are open, area-specific, non-interrogative — they invite the worker's own account; a closed yes/no checklist is not used.

rows.title: Pick-face lighting drops at the far aisle
rows.description: Night-shift pick crew (role-group) report the far-aisle lighting is dim during the second half of the shift, making label scanning slower and riskier.
rows.evidence: Walk conversation — night-shift pick crew (role-labelled, no individual named)

rows.title: Reach-truck pedestrian crossing feels unsafe at shift change
rows.description: Crew raise that the pedestrian crossing by the dispatch door is congested at shift change when reach-trucks and foot traffic overlap.
rows.evidence: Walk conversation — night-shift pick crew + reach-truck operators (role-labelled)

sections.text: Commitments made on the walk (owned, dated, tracked)

rows.action: Survey and restore far-aisle lighting levels to standard on the night-shift pick-face
Re-time reach-truck movements away from the shift-change pedestrian window and re-mark the crossing
Feed both concerns into the next pick-face risk assessment review

sections.text: Every commitment above is an owned, dated action tracked to closure — a walk is not a conversation that evaporates. Any control a commitment implies is ranked by the hierarchy of controls (higher-order first); the crossing re-timing is an engineering/administrative control, not a PPE-only fix.

The count and closure-rate of walk commitments is a leading indicator (KB-DATA-LEADING-INDICATORS — quote source + year). A rising commitment-closure rate is forward evidence of visible leadership and worker engagement; a walk that produces no tracked commitment is recorded as a gap, not signed off as complete.

This is decision-support only and must be reviewed and signed off by a competent person. Worker concerns are role-labelled (psychological safety); no individual is named. Basis: ISO 45001:2018 clauses 5.1 (leadership and commitment) and 5.4 (consultation and participation of workers); HSE HSG65 felt leadership; gemba / genchi-genbutsu method. Next walk cycle and commitment close-out review per the agreed cadence.

items: ISO 45001:2018 §5.4 — consultation and participation of workers
HSE HSG65 — felt leadership / visible safety leadership
